meaning: English rule benefits those who bark (protest).

text: अकटको कुलो, बिकटको खेत, बिसँछु दाजै शिलोकको फेद
meaning: A canal on a slope, a field on a cliff, I remember the base of the Shilok.

text: अकबर राजा, बिरबल काजि, इन्साफ दिन्छन खुसिराजि
meaning: King Akbar, Minister Birbal, happy judge delivers justice.
Akbari gold doesn't need a touchstone to prove its purity.

text: अक्चु खाए बक्छु खाए झुसे बारुलो
meaning: Stung by a wasp? Just eat bitter gourd and eggplant.
A dog hit by a stick is even scared of lightning.
Even a stick doesn't burn without a spark.

text: अघि हेर्नु चार हात, पछि हेर्नु बाह हात
meaning: Look four cubits forward, eight cubits back.
Today's bitterness is tomorrow's sweetness.
The chopping block knows the pain of the chop, but the knife does not. (The wrongdoer never understands the suffering of the victim).

text: अचार खानु मुलाको, संगत गर्नु ठुलाको
meaning: Eat pickles from radish, but keep company with the elders.

text: अच्छा साले जुल्फी पाले, लिखाले टांग हाले
meaning: The good brother-in-law grows long hair, the writer scribbles a note.
God provides food for the python.
A stuck and cracked oil lamp.

text: अदुवा खाई सभामा बस, मुला खाई बन पस
meaning: Eat ginger and sit in the assembly, eat radish and go to the forest.
Mustard during famine, barley during good times.
In famine, give neither man nor food.

text: अनिकालमा लाईफ जोगाउनु, आन्दोलनमा बाईक जोगाउनु
meaning: Save your life during famine, save your bike during a movement.
In famine, eat together sharing whatever you have.

text: अनुहार न दनुवार, पोइ खोज्न थाली
meaning: She has neither the looks nor grace, yet she goes hunting for a husband. ('Danuwar' refers to a native community, not teeth).
Lacking both looks and background, yet demanding a pristine, high-status partner.

text: अन्धाको देशमा आँखा छोप्नु, लङ्गडाको देशमा खुट्टा खोच्याउनु
meaning: In the land of the blind, cover your eyes; in the land of the lame, limp.
The support of the blind is the lame.
A lamp in the hands of a blind person.
A blind man searching for a mirror.
Strength lies in the time of trouble, not in the food.
Never call wealth earned through injustice your own.

text: अभागी मोरो हरुन बस्यो, बाह हातको घाँचो पस्यो
meaning: The unlucky one's peacock flew away, and an eight-cubit splinter got stuck.

text: अभागीको भाई सप्नन्छ, भाग्यमानीको छोरो सप्नन्छ
meaning: The unlucky one's brother sleeps, the lucky one's son sleeps.

text: अराएको काम र भराएको मानोले कति दिन चल्छ
meaning: How long can a borrowed work and a borrowed plow last?

text: अरुको बारिको काँक्रो चोर्नी, आफ्नो भने नुनको ढिको नफोर्नी
meaning: Steal cucumbers from another's garden, but don't break your own salt block.

text: अरूले हाती चढे भनेर आफूले धुरी चढ्नु हुँदैन
meaning: Don't climb a pole just because others ride an elephant.

text: अर्काको आङ्गको जुम्रा देख्ने, आफ्नो आङ्गको भैँसी पनि नदेख्ने
meaning: Noticing a tiny louse on someone else's body, but completely missing the massive buffalo on one's own body.

text: अर्काको गहना लगाउनु, नाक कान दुखाउनु
meaning: Wearing another's jewelry only hurts your nose and ears.
Another's claim becomes a noose around the neck.
Licking another person's calf only leaves your mouth full of hair. (Doing unrewarding work for others brings zero personal benefit).

text: अर्काको बारीमा साग छ, मुटु मिचेर के लाग्छ
meaning: There are greens in another's field, why squeeze your heart for it?

text: आफ्नो टाउको फुटे पिछि अर्काको टाउको बेल
meaning: Only after your own head cracks, you wish another's head had cracked.

text: आफ्नो थैली सुरक्षित राखे अर्कालाई दोष लगाउनु पर्दैन
meaning: Secure your own purse first, then you don't have to blame others.

text: आफ्नो थैलोको गाँठो कस्नु, अरुलाई दोष नदिनु
meaning: Tighten the knot of your own bag, don't blame others.

text: आफ्नो नुन, आफ्नै पानी, खाउ ज्वाई कोखा तानी
meaning: Eat your own salt and water, son-in-law, even if you have to tighten your belt.
One must make their own destiny.

text: आफ्नो रुचि खानु, अर्काको रुचि लाउनु
meaning: Eat according to your own taste, apply what others like.
One's own hand is Lord Jagannath (the ultimate provider).

text: आमा पनि ब्युटी, छोरी पनि ब्युटी, बाउले गर्नुपर्ने चुलाको ड्युटी
meaning: Mother is a beauty, daughter is a beauty, so the poor father is stuck doing stove duty!

text: आमाबाबुको मन छोराछोरीमाथि, छोराछोरीको मन डिजाइनर जुत्तामाथि
meaning: Parents' hearts are set on their children, but the children's minds are entirely on designer shoes.

text: आमा बाबुलाई समस्या टान गाहाँ, छोराछोरीलाई भने स्टाईल नै प्यारो
meaning: Parents find it hard to pull through problems, children only love style.

text: आमा-बाबा को मन छोरा तिर, छोरा को मन अर्काको छोरी तिर
meaning: Father and mother's mind is on their son, the son's mind is on another's daughter.
A mother's love is on the edge of her eyelid.

text: आमाबाबुको मन छोराछोरीमाथि, छोराछोरीको मन ढुङ्गामुढामाथि
meaning: Parents invest their hearts in their kids, but children treat them with the cold indifference of stones and logs.
Mother-son is about care and comfort, father-son is about transactions and obligations.

text: आम्दानी चार आनाको खर्च बाहू आनाको
meaning: Income is four annas, expenditure is twelve annas (living beyond one's means).

When you are blessed with a long life (or an only son whose life you cherish deeply), you get terrified of even a tiny gust of wind or minor ailment. (Being overprotective or excessively anxious about small risks).

text: आयो गयो माया मोह, आएन गएन को हो को हो
meaning: Love and attachment come and go; who is who if they don't come or go?
Eating potatoes but pretending to have the status of a sweet peda.

text: इखालु स्वास्नीको बचन, बहुला कुकुरको दाँत बराबर
meaning: The words of a spiteful, grudging spouse are as dangerous and deeply wounding as the bite of a rabis dog.
Talking about heaven in front of Lord Indra (showing off to the expert).
One forehead, two ropes (torn between two responsibilities).

text: एउटा पोईको दुईटा जोई, खाने बेलामा कोही न कोही
meaning: A husband with two wives ends up neglected; when it's time to cook or serve food, each wife assumes the other handled it, leaving him with nothing.

text: एउटा भए सरासर, दुइटा भए भराभर, तिनवटा भए टाउको फोराफोर
meaning: One is straightforward, two are quarrelsome, three break each other's heads.

text: एउटा सियो चोर अर्को फाली चोर
meaning: One steals a needle, another steals an axe (different scales of wrongdoing).
One person commits a mistake or crime, but their entire family lineage or community has to suffer the painful consequences.
Passed from one ear to two ears, and suddenly it's out in the open field. (A secret known by more than one person will quickly spread to the whole world).

text: एक कानले सुन्यो, अर्को कानले उडायो
meaning: Heard with one ear, let it fly out the other.

text: एक घर विष्ट, दश घर कामी, जस्ता तिमी उस्तै हामी
meaning: With only one house of a high-caste Bisht surrounded by ten houses of blacksmith artisans, everyone ends up living similarly. (In close communities, people inevitably adapt to match each other's status and lifestyle).

text: एक छाक खाने जोगी, दुई छाक खाने भोगी, तीन छाक खाने रोगी
meaning: Eating once a day is a hermit, twice a day is a householder, thrice a day is a patient.

text: एक थुकी सुकी, सय थुकी नदी
meaning: One spit dries up, a hundred spits become a river.
A thief once is a thief forever.
One path, two tasks (two birds with one stone).
A single month of Magh does not exhaust the entire winter. (A single temporary victory doesn't mean hardships are over forever, or a single act of kindness doesn't mean you won't need help again).

text: एक रुपैंया धन छ, घोडा किन्छु भन्छ
meaning: He has only one rupee but says he'll buy a horse.
One hand alone cannot produce a clapping sound. (It takes two sides to cause a quarrel, or cooperation from both parties to achieve a result).
Unity is strength.
Storm on one side, suspension bridge on the other.
A solitary Brihaspati (the god of wisdom) is easily outvoted and dismissed as a liar. (No matter how wise or right you are, a single voice cannot win against an overwhelming, united crowd).

text: ओखरबोटमा चिल बस्यो, देखेपछि दिल बस्यो
meaning: A kite bird perched on the walnut tree, and the moment I saw it, my heart fell in love. (A traditional romantic rhyming couplet about instant infatuation).

text: ओरालो लागेको मृगलाई बाच्छाले पनि खेद्छ
meaning: Even a calf chases a deer that is going downhill.
When offered just a finger, they try to swallow the whole wrist. (Give someone an inch and they will take a mile; taking unfair advantage of kindness).
Neither effective medicine nor good, honest advice ever tastes sweet. (The truth and things that heal you are often uncomfortable to swallow).
Buy on the new moon and throw away on the full moon.

text: कङ्गालको जात हुँदैन, बोक्सीको गाउँ हुँदैन
meaning: A desperately poor person loses their social standing/caste privileges, and a malicious person (metaphorically called a witch) belongs to no single village because they are driven away from everywhere.
An inexperienced doctor's dose is a journey to the land of death.
Where did you get so completely lost, like a lump of fresh butter melting away into clarified butter? (Said to someone who vanishes completely during a task or gets absorbed in a confusing mess).
Some people enjoy stacks upon stacks of massive wealth, while others are left staring at empty hands, cursing their destiny. (Highlighting extreme economic inequality).
A tiny stream of urine feels like a catastrophic, life-threatening landslide to an ant. (Something trivial to a powerful person can completely ruin someone small and vulnerable).
Applying medicine for a headache on the navel.
The topknot is shorter than the hair.
A tiny stream of urine feels like a catastrophic, life-threatening landslide to an ant. (Something trivial to a powerful person can completely ruin someone small and vulnerable).
Eating ghee wrapped in a blanket (hiding wealth while enjoying it).
Using a massive wood-cutting axe just to chop soft taro leaves. (Using excessive, unnecessary force or an oversized tool to solve a tiny, simple problem).
The state of the modern dark age (Kali Yuga) where superficiality and physical exposure take precedence over substance and virtue.
A goat crying in front of a butcher.

text: कसाईको अगाडी बाख्राको आँसुको के अर्थ
meaning: What does a goat's tear mean in front of a butcher?

When looking at a hard, round rice-flour sweet (Kasar), it is impossible to separate what is the outer crust and what is the inner core. (Said of things or people that are uniform, solid, or equally stubborn throughout).
One man's food is another man's poison.

text: कसैलाई के धन्दा घरज्वाईलाई खानको धन्दा
meaning: The only worry for a live-in son-in-law is worry about food.
Treating some people with immense favor (giving them lakhs) while banishing others to the margins (putting them out on the steep slope). (Showing blatant favoritism and unfair partiality).

text: कसैले तपाईं भनोस् नभनोस्, आफैं मपाईं
meaning: Whether anyone shows you respect or calls you 'sir' or not, you go around loudly blowing your own trumpet. (Being completely full of yourself and self-absorbed).

text: कस्ता कस्ता कहाँ गए मुसाको बच्चा दरबारमा
meaning: All kinds have gone; a mouse's child is in the palace.

Where do you think you can swim off to, little fish? You will ultimately end up right inside my wicker trap. (Said when someone tries to escape or outsmart you, but you know they have absolutely no choice but to return to you).

An absolute mockery or nonsensical event happening at the Royal Nepal Academy (Pragya Pratisthan). (A modern satirical twist on the classic Handigaun proverb, used to mock bureaucratic or political absurdities).
A bizarre festival found nowhere else but in Handigaun. (Used to describe an utterly unique, chaotic, or completely backward way of doing things that defies all logic).

text: काका भन्नु र कान्छा बाऊ भन्नु एउटै कुरो हो
meaning: Calling someone uncle and calling them youngest father is the same thing.

text: काग कराउँदै गर्छ, पिना सुक्दै गर्छ
meaning: The crow keeps cawing, but the oil-cake (पिना) keeps drying anyway. (Critics will keep making noise, but important work must go on uninterrupted. 'पिना' is leftover mustard cake used for fertilizer/feed, not an anvil).
When the crow sits, the branch breaks.

text: काग बाठो भयो भने गु खान्छ
meaning: If the crow becomes clever, it eats feces.
The cuckoo is smarter than the crow.
A crow erases the name of a crow.
An egret among crows.

text: कागलाई बेल पाक्यो, हर्ष न विस्मात्
meaning: The wood apple is ripe for the crow, neither joy nor sorrow.
The crow calls the cuckoo black.
For a one-eyed ox, there is no new moon or full moon.

text: काम कुरो एकातिर, कुम्लो बोकी ठिमीतिर
meaning: Leaving the actual important business behind and heading off to Thimi with one's luggage. (Completely abandoning your primary duty to pursue an irrelevant side distraction).

text: काम गर्दा नोकरले जसरी, खाना खाँदा राजाले जसरी
meaning: Work like a servant, eat like a king.

text: काम गर्ने कालु, मकै खाने भालु
meaning: Kalu does all the hard labor in the fields, but the wild bear comes and eats all the corn. (One person does all the hard work while someone else swoops in and steals the credit or rewards).

text: काम गर्ने हनुमान, पगडी कस्ने ढेँडु
meaning: Lord Hanuman does all the heavy lifting and fighting, but the lazy black-faced monkey (ढेँडु) gets to wear the celebratory turban. (Similar to the bear and corn proverb—exploiting someone else's hard work for your own glory).

text: काम नपाएकी बुहारी, पाडाको कण्डो कन्याएर बसी
meaning: A daughter-in-law with absolutely no work to do ends up sitting around scratching a male buffalo calf's backside. (Being so utterly idle that you waste time on completely pointless, unproductive tasks).

text: काम छउञ्जेल भाँडो, काम सकिएपछि ठाँडो
meaning: Treating a tool or a person like a precious vessel while you need them, but throwing them out or standing them up coldly the second the job is done. (Using people purely for selfish utility).
No work or duty, but an enemy of the grain.
No work or shelter, but the stomach is full of food.

text: काम पनि छैन दाम पनि छैन
meaning: No work, no money either.

text: काम पाइनस् बुहारी, पाडाको ढाड कन्याइदे
meaning: If you can't find any real work to do, daughter-in-law, just go scratch the calf's back. (A variation of ID 117, mocking someone who keeps busy with completely useless tasks).

text: काम साँचे आफूलाई, खान साँचे अरूलाई
meaning: Save work for yourself, save food for others.
The blacksmith's turn comes in Kartik.
A blacksmith's wedding has eighteen problems.

Let's pull our resources together, look past minor differences, and enjoy a hearty chicken feast. (A colloquial saying about getting together with friends/partners to share profits, often used with a slight hint of a shady alliance).
Black printed letters look like nothing more than a big black buffalo to someone who cannot read. (Being completely illiterate).
A black cloud doesn't always produce rain.

text: कालो बाहून, गोरो शुद्र, यिनीदेखि डराउँछन् महारुद्र
meaning: Dark Brahmin, light Shudra; even Lord Shiva is afraid of them.
Taking the road through Kuti (Tibet) just to go southwest to Kashi/Varanasi (India). (Taking a completely roundabout, highly inefficient, or backward route to reach a destination).
Why are you crying, Mangale? Because of your own foolish actions. (You brought this misery completely upon yourself, so don't blame anyone else).

text: किनेको दाउरा किनेकै पानी, कर्मचारीका श्रीमती महलका रानी
meaning: Bought firewood has its own water; a clerk's wife is a palace queen.

text: कुकुर भुक्दै गर्छ, हाती लम्केँदै गर्छ
meaning: The dog keeps barking, the elephant keeps walking.

text: कुकुरको पुच्छर बा्रह वर्ष ढुङ्ग्रोमा राखे पनि बाङ्गाको बाङ्गै
meaning: A dog's tail remains completely crooked even if you keep it trapped straight inside a pipe (ढुङ्ग्रो) for twelve years. (Old habits die hard; a person with an inherently bad character will never change, no matter how much you try to reform them).
Meat is a delicacy for a dog.
A dog cannot digest ghee.
Mange for a dog.

text: कुकुरले खुट्टा टोक्यो भन्दैमा कुकुरको खुट्टा टोक्नु हुन्न
meaning: Just because a dog bit your leg, you don't bite the dog's leg.
A bone is a delicacy for a dog.

The exact heap of trash that you were planning to sweep away with a broom got blown away cleanly by a sudden gust of wind. (When an annoying problem or an unwanted person gets solved naturally by external circumstances without you having to lift a finger).
The daughter-in-law from that specific household who got caught greedily munching on roasted soybeans. (Mocking someone who sneaks around eating food or acts cheap behind closed doors).
The local deity of Kunta who is permanently covered in ash. (A humorous or dismissive reference to someone who looks unkempt or stays in a humble, modest state).

text: कुब्जी बुढीलाई उठी सुख न बसी सुख
meaning: For a hunchbacked old woman, there is absolute discomfort whether she stands up or sits down. (Being trapped in a miserable dilemma where every single available choice brings pain or difficulty).

text: कुमालेको चक्र घुमी फिरी त्यहीं आउँछ
meaning: The potter's wheel keeps spinning and comes back to the same place.

text: कुरो र कुलो जता लग्यो त्यतै जान्छ
meaning: A conversation and a canal go wherever you lead them.
Playing auspicious festive wedding music (Panche Baja) during a highly unlucky moment or a funeral. (Doing something completely inappropriate or poorly timed for the given occasion).
What are you looking for, one-eyed man?
What are you looking for, leader? A chair.

text: केटाकेटी आए, गुलेली खेलाए, मट्याङ्ग्राको सत्यानाश
meaning: Children arrived, played wildly with their slingshots, and completely wasted all the clay pellets (मट्याङ्ग्रा). (Immature people taking over a serious project and completely wasting valuable resources through reckless play).

text: केटी हेर्न नपर्ने, केटो बोल्ने नपर्ने
meaning: A girl shouldn't have to look, a boy shouldn't have to speak.
A Khas's words are like a knife in the stomach.

text: खसी जोख्नु सय चोटि, मान्छे जोख्नु एक चोटि
meaning: Weigh a goat a hundred times, but weigh a person just once.

Showing off a premium castrated goat but baiting and switching it for a cheap pumpkin (फर्सी). (A classic bait-and-switch scam; promising something high quality but delivering something practically worthless).

text: खसी भनूँ बोसो छैन, बोको भनूँ नसो छैन
meaning: Can't call it a choice castrated goat because it has zero fat, yet can't call it a breeding buck because it has no strength or stamina. (Describing something of exceptionally poor quality that fails to meet any standard or category).
Eat or wear, after death it's all useless.

text: खाई न पाई छालाको टोपी लाई
meaning: Neither ate nor got, but puts on a leather cap.
Neither ate nor got, but wears a tie over the gown.

text: खाउँ कर्कली, पेट फर्कली, नखाउँ कर्कली, सासू झर्कली
meaning: If I eat the stir-fried greens, my stomach turns; if I don't, my mother-in-law is annoyed.
Eat it if you want, otherwise force-feed yourself. (Take it or leave it; a blunt, uncompromising offer where you have no choice but to accept what is given).

text: खान जाने औषधि, खान नजाने विष
meaning: What is medicine for one is poison for another.

text: खाने पिउने रामे, चोट पाउने चामे
meaning: Rame does the eating and drinking, Chame gets the beating.

text: खाने बेलामा घिन नसम्झुनु, सुन्ने बेलामा ऋण नसम्झुनु
meaning: Don't remember disgust while eating, don't remember debt while listening.
A mustache does not block the eating mouth.
Refuse out of custom, but after eating, four mana.

Once you happily consume the sweet liquid molasses (खुदो), your face naturally bends downward in shame or compliance. (Accepting a bribe or a big favor makes you completely lose your moral authority to speak up against that person).

Feeding someone a delicious rice pudding (खीर) only to wash their mouth out with dirty mud afterward. (SPOILING a great favor or act of kindness by following it up with an unprovoked insult or cruel treatment).
If you have feet, you can walk the whole world.
If you have feet, there are a thousand shoes.
The feast is incomplete without chili pepper.

text: खुस्किन लागेको हाकिमलाई पिउनले पनि लखेट्छ
meaning: An officer who is about to be fired or lose his authority gets treated with utter disrespect and chased around even by the office peon (पिउन). (When a powerful leader loses their official position, even their lowest subordinates stop fearing or respecting them).
Scare the farmhand, terrify the guest.

text: खेताला लाए छैन काम, छोराछोरी पठाए आधा काम, आफू गए पुरै काम
meaning: Hiring a laborer does no work; sending children does half work; going yourself does full work.
One's own farming is good, another's wife is good.
Crossed the river but forgot the stick.
Rivers and love flow downward.
In a land of rivers, there is shortage of water.
A donkey does not get up until the load is on.
A thundering cloud does not rain.

text: गहिराको घरलाई पहिराको डर, डाँडाको घरलाई जाडाको डर
meaning: A house in a deep gorge fears a landslide; a house on a hill fears the cold.
Leave the morsel, but do not leave the company.
A cow is equal to the Ganges.

text: गाउँ भरिको साईनो, पानी खान पाइन
meaning: All the relatives in the village, but no water to drink.

There isn't a single drop of water left in the water vessel, yet they are making a curry (तिहुन) overflowing with excessive watery gravy. (Mismanaging scarce resources or putting on a grand, fake display when you lack basic essentials).
A crow's wing in front of a vulture.
A singer is known by his whistling.

text: गु खानेले गु खाए पनि सुँघ्नेले नसुँघ्नु
meaning: Even if an unhygienic person is dirty enough to consume filth, bystanders shouldn't stoop down to look at or smell it. (If someone is doing something incredibly shameless or corrupt, decent people should distance themselves completely rather than getting involved).

text: गु पुछेको टालो पनि बेलामा काम लाग्छ
meaning: Even a rag once used to clean up filth can find a specific use at the right emergency moment. (Nothing and nobody is completely useless in this world; everything has value given the right context).
If you move feces, it stinks.

text: गुन्द्रुकले गुन्द्रुक खान्छ, तँलाई म खान्छु
meaning: Fermented leafy greens can be mixed with more greens, but I am going to completely destroy you. (A fierce, aggressive declaration of dominance over an opponent. 'गुन्द्रुक' is fermented mustard/radish leaves, not virtue).

text: गुन्द्रुक पकाउनु पर्दैन, साली फकाउनु पर्दैन
meaning: You don't need elaborate cooking skills to boil basic fermented leafy greens (गुन्द्रुक), and you don't need heavy coaxing to get along with a friendly sister-in-law (साली). (Certain things in life are naturally easy, straightforward, and require minimal effort).
Fermented leafy greens (गुन्द्रुक) don't require complex, delicate culinary preparation. (Referring to something that is simple, rustic, and ready to go).

text: गुरु मान्नु बुझेर, बिहे गर्नु रोजेर, पानी खानु छानेर, कुरो गर्नु जानेर
meaning: Respect a teacher wisely, marry by choice, drink water after filtering, speak with knowledge.
The master is raw molasses (गुल/गुँड), but the disciple refines themselves into pure white sugar. (When a student completely surpasses their own teacher in knowledge, skill, or achievements).

If you stir up a pile of filth, it's only going to release a terrible stench. (Digging into an old, dirty scandal or arguing with a toxic person will only bring shame and trouble upon you; it's best left alone).

Urinating on your own feet provides a brief, immediate sensation of warmth, but leaves you cold, wet, and miserable seconds later. (Opting for a short-sighted, temporary fix that makes your long-term problem significantly worse).

text: गोरु नभएको हली र ठेडी नभएको हलोको के काम
meaning: What good is a plowman without an ox, or a plow without a beam?

text: गोलीको घाउ निको हुन्छ, बोली को हुँदैन
meaning: A bullet wound heals, but a wound from words never does.

She has no stable home or family lineage to speak of, yet she boasts a massive desire for a fancy husband (पोइ). (Having grand, unrealistic ambitions when you lack even the most basic foundations of stability).

text: घर नखाएकी छोरी, परदेश नखाएको छोरो
meaning: A daughter who hasn't mastered running her own household, and a son who hasn't faced the grit of working abroad. (Describing spoiled, sheltered children who haven't experienced real-world struggles).

text: घर नभएको छोरो र सुत्केरी नभएकी बुहारी
meaning: A son who hasn't built a home foundation and a daughter-in-law who hasn't gone through postpartum care. (Referring to a young, inexperienced household that hasn't faced life's major milestones yet).
A tiger at home, a cat in the jungle.

text: घरमा चामल सकिएको दिन एकादशी, गोजीमा पैसो सकिएको दिन औंसी
meaning: The day rice runs out at home is Ekadashi; the day money runs out is new moon.

text: घरमा छैन तेल, खान खोज्छ सेल
meaning: No oil at home, but wants to eat a fried bread.
Not even a piece of cheap chaff (भुस) at home to burn, yet carrying an expensive cigarette in the pocket. (Showing off a luxury lifestyle when you can't even afford the absolute bare essentials).

text: घरै पिँडालु, वनै पिँडालु, ससुराली गयो बा्रह हातको पिँडालु
meaning: Sick of eating taro (पिँडालु) at home, sick of finding taro in the forest, only to visit the in-laws and be served a massive, twelve-cubit-long taro! (Trying to escape an annoying, repetitive situation only to find an even bigger version of it elsewhere).

text: घरै पोले पछि खरानीको के पिर
meaning: After burning the house, who cares about the ash?

Swallow a bone only after measuring the size of your throat. (Live strictly within your means; do not take on liabilities or expenses bigger than your actual capacity. 'हाइनेक' or high-neck sweater is a modern slang parody of this original proverb).
Swallow a bone according to your throat.

text: घिउ न तेल, पका बुढी सेल
meaning: With absolutely no ghee or oil in the kitchen, the old man demands the wife to cook deep-fried ring bread (सेल रोटी). (Demanding results or high-quality outputs when you haven't provided any of the necessary raw materials or resources).
They don't even know how to eat properly, yet they loudly blame the cook for their dissatisfaction. (A bad workman blames his tools; blaming others for your own incompetence).
After traveling all over, you end up right back where you started at Rumjatar. (Going through a long, exhausting process or argument only to return to the exact same starting point or status quo).

Planting upland, dry-soil rice (घैया) won't magically yield premium lowland paddy (धान). (You reap what you sow; you cannot expect premium, high-quality results if you put in cheap or improper foundations).
A person who rides a horse eventually falls.
Where did the ghee spill? In the dal.

A restless, disobedient ox ends up getting branded with a hot iron in four different places. (An overly hyperactive, troublesome person will constantly run into trouble and carry the scars/punishments of their bad behavior).
Gifting a sacred rosary of Rudraksha beads to a completely merciless, wicked person (चण्डाल). (Giving holy or highly valuable things to someone who has zero capacity to appreciate or respect them).
The overfilled pot of arrogance will eventually crack and shatter. (Pride always comes before a total fall).
Smoke hash, but don't lose heart.

text: चाँदो लाग्ने आगो र मुख लाग्ने नोकर
meaning: Fire that catches easily and a servant who talks back.
Don't cast the net without knowing the current.

text: चित्तको पनि लोभ, पापाको पनि लोभ
meaning: Greedily wanting to keep the beautifully patterned coin (चित्त - heads/tails) while also wanting the sweet candy (पापा). (Wanting to have your cake and eat it too; trying to greedily claim two mutually exclusive choices).

text: चिताले मरेपछि जलाउँछ, चिन्ताले जिउँदै जलाउँछ
meaning: A funeral pyre (चिता) only burns your physical body after you are dead, but excessive worry (चिन्ता) consumes and burns you completely while you are still alive.
A leopard has a thousand pens.

text: चितुवाको भात भाते होइन, स्वास्नीको जात जाते होइन
meaning: A leopard's sporadic meal cannot be relied upon as regular sustenance, just as traditional patriarchy historically biases against relying on a woman's changing marital clan identity. (An old social commentary on unstable or unreliable situations).

text: चिनीको नाम जपेर मुख गुलियो हुदैन
meaning: Chanting the name of sugar does not sweeten the mouth.
Neither know nor recognize, but ask for a push.

text: चीनको राजा, भोटको नुन, ज्यानै दिए नि नदेख्ने गुन
meaning: The distant Emperor of China and the essential salt traded from Tibet; no matter how much effort you invest, certain cold systems or ungrateful people will never acknowledge your sacrifices.

Selling off your highly productive farming ox in the peak month of Chaitra just to buy a heavy wool blanket (पाखी) right as summer starts. (Making an incredibly foolish, poorly timed financial decision that trades a long-term asset for an unseasonal, useless item).

text: चोर औँला अरूतिर, बाँकी औँला आफैँतिर
meaning: When you point your index finger (चोर औँला) to accuse someone else, remember that the remaining three fingers on your hand are pointing right back at you. (Before you judge or blame others, look at your own massive faults).

text: चोरको खुट्टा काट् भन्दा खुट्टा उचाल्ने
meaning: The moment someone yells out 'Chop off the thief's leg!', the actual guilty thief instinctively flinches and lifts his foot up. (A guilty conscience needs no accuser; a criminal will easily give themselves away out of paranoia).
A thief's friend is a thief.

text: चोरको स्वर ठूलो, डॉकाको ऑखा ठूलो
meaning: The thief has a loud voice, the robber has big eyes.
A thief has a thousand tricks.

A comfortable resting platform (चौतारो) is awarded to the criminal, while the honest, innocent saint is condemned to the execution stake (सुली). (Describing a completely corrupt, broken system where wrongdoers are celebrated and the innocent are punished).

text: चोरले अरुलाई पनि चोर नै देख्छ
meaning: A thief sees everyone else as a thief.

text: चौटा खान गएकी बुढी झोलमा डुबेर मरी
meaning: The greedy old woman who snuck out specifically to grab a solid chunk of meat (चौटा) ended up slipping and drowning right inside the meat broth. (Being driven by intense greed can easily blind you and lead to your total ruin).

text: जट्टु छोरालाई पाटी, लिँडे छोरालाई घुम
meaning: The clever, reliable son gets the public rest-house property to manage, while the stubborn, useless son (लिँडे) gets left out with nothing but a rustic woven rain-shield (घुम). (Distributing family inheritance or responsibilities based entirely on a child's competence and behavior).

text: छन् गेडी सबै मेरी, छैनन् गेडी सबै टेढी
meaning: As long as you have money/grains (गेडी) in your pocket, everyone claims to be your best friend; the moment you run out, those exact same people turn their backs on you and treat you coldly.

text: छाडेको पोइ र हिडेको स्वास्नीको नाम कसले लिन्छ
meaning: Who takes the name of a left husband and a walked-away wife?

text: छातीमा राखी कसले के नै लैजानु छ र
meaning: Keeping it on the chest, what can anyone take away?
The skin may go, but the black does not go away.
Jasmine oil on a shrew's head.

text: छेत्री बिग्रियो मोजले, नेवार बिग्रियो भोजले
meaning: A Chhetri is spoiled by pleasure, a Newar is spoiled by feasts.

text: छेपारोको मन्त्र नजान्ने, साँपको दुलामा हात घुसाउने
meaning: Not even knowing the basic protective spell against a harmless lizard, yet recklessly shoving your entire arm straight into a venomous snake's hole. (An absolute fool with zero skills taking on an incredibly dangerous task far beyond their depth).

text: छोरि जन्मे ज्वाईलाई, छोरो जन्मे बुहारीलाई
meaning: When a daughter is born, it's for the son-in-law; when a son is born, it's for the daughter-in-law.

Scolding or beating one's own daughter publicly just to send an indirect, firm warning to the newly arrived daughter-in-law to stay disciplined. (Reprimanding a close insider to subtly teach an outsider a lesson without causing a direct confrontation).

text: छोरीको बैंस बाबुले होइन अरुले देख्छन्
meaning: A daughter's youth is seen by others, not by her father.

text: छोरो उताउलो हूँदा घुँडा भाच्छ, छोरी उताउली हूँदा पोइला जान्छे
meaning: When a son is impatient, he breaks his knee; when a daughter is impatient, she goes to her husband's home.
Mangal in the jungle.
Wherever the slope goes, there goes the Ganges.

text: जता काफल पाक्छ, त्यता चरी नाच्छ
meaning: Wherever the Kafal fruit ripens, the bird dances there.
Wherever there is rice pudding, there is a crowd.

text: जति खान नपाएर मर्दैनन्, त्योभन्दा बढी खान नजानेर
meaning: Fewer people die from actual starvation than those who die from overeating or not knowing how to maintain a healthy, balanced diet. (A warning against greed, gluttony, and bad lifestyle habits).
The farther the distance, the deeper the love.
The more consistently you milk a cow, the more milk she yields. (The more effort, practice, or care you put into a productive resource, the more benefits it will continue to provide).
As many pots, so many splashes.
As many cooks, so many khichdis.
Republic for the people, neither joy nor sorrow.

text: जब भयो राती, तब बुढी ताती
meaning: When night falls, then the old woman warms up.

text: जलै विष्णु, थलै विष्णु, फुटेपछि कहाँ राख्नु
meaning: Lord Vishnu resides in the sacred water, and Lord Vishnu resides on the land; but once this fragile earthen clay pot shatters, where on earth are you going to store anything? (A philosophical reflection on the human body or a delicate relationship—once broken completely, it can no longer hold value).

text: जसका छोरा उसका धन, जसका भैंसी उसका बन
meaning: He who has sons has wealth; he who has buffaloes has the forest.

text: जसकी आमा भान्छे, उसको छोरो भोकै
meaning: Even though his own mother is the head cook in the kitchen, the poor son ironically ends up going completely hungry. (The shoemaker's children often go barefoot; those closest to a rich resource or provider are sometimes the most neglected).

text: जसको छैन एक फाँक सुती, उसैले लगाउँछ दस लाखको फुर्ती
meaning: A person who doesn't even own a single strip of basic cotton fabric (सुती) goes around bragging with the arrogance of a multi-millionaire. (Empty vessels make the loudest noise; insecure people putting on a grand, fake show).
Whose drum it is, his rhythm.
Whose sword it is, his home and property.
He who owns the monkey makes it dance.
Whose stick it is, his buffalo.

text: जसको सिता खानु, उसको गीता गाउनु
meaning: Whose rice you eat, his song you sing.
As it came, so it went.

text: जसले बोक्छ भारी, उसैले खान्छ मकै मारी
meaning: The hard-working laborer who actually shoulders the heavy burdens deserves to sit down and enjoy the roasted corn. (The person who does the real, exhausting work should be the one reaping the direct rewards).

text: जसले पैसालाई चिन्दैन, तेस्लाई पैसाले पनी चिन्दैन
meaning: He who does not respect money, money does not respect him either.

text: जसले मह काढ्छ, उसले हात चाट्छ
meaning: He who extracts honey licks his hand.

text: जापानीले बेसार खाओस् न स्वाद पाओस्
meaning: Let the Japanese eat turmeric, maybe he'll taste it.

text: जसो जसो बराजु उसे उसे नाति
meaning: The more modest the elder, the more generous the grandson.

text: जसो जसो बाहुन बाजे, उसे उसे स्वाहा
meaning: The more like a Brahmin the grandfather, the more Swaha to him.

text: जित पाल्न भन्दा नाम पाल्न गाहो
meaning: It is harder to maintain a name than to maintain a victory.

text: जिउँदी कमारीको बाह्रबिसे पोइ, मरेकी कमारीको कोही न कोही
meaning: As long as the female servant is alive and working, a dozen men chase after her claiming to be her husband; the second she passes away, absolutely nobody steps forward to care for her. (People line up around you when you are useful, but abandon you entirely when you can no longer benefit them).

People gladly join your wedding procession when you win, but they only show up as somber funeral mourners when you lose. (Fair-weather friends who celebrate your victories but vanish during your struggles).

text: जिते हस्तिनापुरको राज, हारे चपरी मुनीको बास
meaning: Win and rule Hastinapur, lose and live under a thatch.

text: जुत्ता लगाउनेको चुरिफुरी खोला तर्दा देखिन्छ
meaning: The immense arrogance and stylish boasting of someone wearing fancy shoes gets completely tested the second they hit a rushing river and have to cross. (A person's fake status or superficial confidence crumbles the moment they encounter a real, practical obstacle).

text: जुत्ताको माला लगाएको नेता छाला देख्दा तर्सन्छ
meaning: A leader wearing a garland of shoes gets scared seeing leather.

text: जुन गोरुको सिंग छैन उसैको नाम तिखे
meaning: The ox that has no horns is named Tikhay.
Whichever hermit comes, his ear is already pierced.

text: जुन थालमा खायो, त्यही थालमा हग्छ
meaning: He eats in the same plate he defecates.

text: जुन नेता आए पनि घुस खान नछाड्ने
meaning: Whichever leader comes, he does not stop taking bribes.
The light of a firefly does not brighten heaven.
Do not count wealth earned from gambling.
The spade in elder brother's hand is easy.

text: जेठी बुढीको राम्रो कुरा तितो, कान्छीको चाहिँ झटारो नै मिठो
meaning: The good advice of the first wife is bitter, but even a quarrel with the youngest wife is sweet.

The very leader appointed to guide the crowd safely down the path turns around and defecates right in the middle of it. (When the person in charge of a project or country is the exact one sabotaging it through corrupt or disgusting actions).
The thief himself has the loudest voice.
The person who succumbs to absolute fear is as good as dead. (He who hesitates or panics in the face of danger loses the battle entirely).

text: जो निर्धन उसैको नाम धन प्रसाद
meaning: He who is poor, his name is Dhan Prasad.

The very malicious person accused of being a destructive witch turns out to be the local birth midwife (सुँडेनी). (The wolf in sheep's clothing; when the person trusted to protect and deliver you is secretly the one trying to harm you).
Whoever's hand, that is the support.
He who is short, a spike in his mouth.

text: जोईको जोई, पोइको पोइ, हामी बीचमा कोही न कोही
meaning: A dedicated wife belongs to her husband, and a husband belongs to his wife; nobody else has any right to interfere in their sacred, private bond.

text: जोगी देख्दा भैंसी तर्सिने, भैंसी देख्दा जोगी तर्सिने
meaning: Buffalo gets scared seeing a hermit, hermit gets scared seeing a buffalo.
A hermit is a guest at a hermit's house.
A hippie is a guest at a hermit's house.

As long as the vine (झाल) survives and stays healthy, young baby vegetables (चिचिला) will keep growing on it. (As long as the core foundation or main source of a business remains intact, it will keep producing small profits or results).

An elephant does not drop dead just because a petty housefly brags or acts tough (डिंगा). (A powerful, established person or organization won't be harmed in the slightest by the empty threats of weak critics).

Squashing a tiny housefly doesn't yield a single standard unit of heavy weight (Dharni or Bisauli). (Spending immense energy, time, and effort on a petty task that results in absolutely zero tangible profit).

text: झुकेको शिर र भत्केको भिरप्रति कसैको आश हुँदैन
meaning: Nobody places their future hopes or trust in a defeated person hanging their head in shame, nor on an unstable, crumbling landslide cliff. (People avoid investing their time or trust in failing ventures or broken individuals).
A liar takes an oath quickly.

A disposable leaf-plate (टपरी) can never be washed clean for reuse because its nature is to be used once and thrown away. (Certain fundamentally flawed things, toxic systems, or bad habits cannot be reformed or made pure no matter how much cosmetic cleaning you do).

text: टपरीमा भात, हातमा सिन्को, निर्वाह भयो आजको दिनको
meaning: A basic meal of rice served on a simple leaf plate with just a sharp wooden toothpick (सिन्को) in hand; and just like that, another day's survival is managed. (Being completely content with a humble, modest lifestyle that covers bare daily needs).
Just because it shines does not make it gold.

text: टाढाको देउताभन्दा नजिकको भुत काम लाग्छ
meaning: A terrifying local ghost right next door is far more useful in an emergency than a benevolent god living on a distant mountain peak. (A reliable, nearby neighbor or resource—even if imperfect—is much better during a crisis than a powerful helper who is too far away to reach you).

Worshipping a grand, faraway deity while completely ignoring or mistreating the immediate spirit right in your own backyard/garden (करेसा). (Praising strangers or distant entities while neglecting the very people close to you who actually support your daily life).

Grabbing someone firmly by their topknot of hair (टुप्पी) to completely immobilize them before dealing a harsh slap to the cheek. (Taking complete control of an opponent's weak point to dominate and humiliate them without giving them a chance to escape).

text: टुप्पीभन्दा माथि थात छैन, बाहुनभन्दा माथि जात छैन
meaning: There is no physical crown or property higher than the topknot of your head, just as traditional orthodox systems placed no caste higher than a Brahmin. (A traditional saying describing the absolute peak or ultimate ceiling of a hierarchy).
Even an orphan's day turns around.
God is the only support of an orphan.

text: टोपी न सोपी, कपाल केले छोपी
meaning: With absolutely no cap (टोपी) or proper headwear to speak of, what on earth are you going to use to cover your messy hair? (Lacking the basic tools or preparation required to successfully hide a glaring flaw or handle a task).
No place or fix, an old woman's desire.

text: ठाउँमा लगाए गाजल, कुठाउँमा लगाए मोसो
meaning: Applied beautifully around the eyes, it is praised as elegant cosmetic eyeliner (गाजल); but smeared carelessly anywhere else on the face, it looks like dirty black soot (मोसो). (An action, asset, or word is only valuable and beautiful if used in the correct context; otherwise, it becomes a complete embarrassment).

text: ठाडो टीका लाउँछे, स्वामी कहाँ धाउँछे
meaning: She puts on a vertical sectarian forehead mark (typical of an unmarried ascetic or specific tradition) yet desperately runs around looking for a husband. (A direct contradiction between someone's external religious appearance and their internal worldly desires; hypocrisy).

text: ठेस नलागी उखुबारी पस्नु, ऋण नलागी जमानी बस्नु
meaning: Walking blindly into a dense sugarcane field guarantees you will get scraped and poked, just as signing up to be a financial guarantor (जमानी) for someone else guarantees you will inherit their heavy debt. (A strong warning against foolishly taking on liability for others).

You don't need a massive, steep mountain cliff to trip and stub your toe—a tiny, overlooked stone on a flat path will do it. (Ruin or failure doesn't always come from massive crises; it's often the small, ignored mistakes that bring you down).
Don't enter the sugarcane field without getting a push.

Serving a sweet bread prepared with liquid molasses (खुदो) to someone whose mouth is already badly burned. (Trying to patch up a deep, painful insult or loss with a superficial, sweet favor that cannot be enjoyed).
Fearful people have disgusting words.

text: डराउने मान्छेलाई रुखको ठुटो पनि बाघ बन्छ
meaning: For a fearful person, even a tree stump becomes a tiger.

text: डाँकाले खाएको धन, आगोले खाएको बन
meaning: Wealth eaten by a robber, forest eaten by fire.

A known liar's grand dinner invitation can only be trusted as real after you have actually sat down, eaten the food, and digested it. (Never trust the promises of a habitual deceiver until the results have fully materialized).

Thick cornmeal porridge (ढिँडो) and watery flour soup (फाँडो) are essentially made of the exact same basic ingredients. (Two different options that look slightly different on the surface but are fundamentally identical in substance or quality).

text: ढिकी जाँदा स्वर्गमा पनि धानै कुट्छ
meaning: Even if an wooden foot-pounder (ढिकी) is magically transported all the way to heaven, it will still spend its entire existence doing nothing but pounding raw paddy. (A person who cannot escape their hard-wired habits, low skill level, or labor-intensive mindset even when placed in a luxurious or superior environment).

Let it take a long time, but let the child born be a son. (An old patriarchal saying traditionally used to emphasize that waiting patiently for a desired, premium outcome is better than rushing into a quick disappointment).
While searching for a stone, found a god.

text: ढुंगाको भर माटो, माटोको भर ढुंगा
meaning: Soil depends on stone, stone depends on soil.

text: धुतेकी छोरी कनकाको खानेमा परी, कनका देखेर उफ्रेर मरी
meaning: A spoiled, pretentious daughter who used to look down on simple food ended up married into a home where they only eat broken rice grains (कनिका); upon seeing it, she threw a massive tantrum. (Someone who put on heavy airs of superiority getting a harsh, humbling reality check by fate).
You dare, I will get it there.

text: तँ आउँदा मोही पाइनस्, तेरो कमारी पठाइदे दहि दिउँला
meaning: When you came to my house yourself, there wasn't even a drop of basic buttermilk (मोही) available for you; yet you have the nerve to ask me to send your servant back later promising I'll give her premium yogurt (दही)! (Mocking someone who makes grand, empty promises of future luxury when they can't even deliver the bare minimum right now).

text: तँ कुटे जस्तो गर, म रोए जस्तो गर्छु
meaning: You do as if you beat me, I will do as if I cry.

Leaving a beautiful young woman as a trusted deposit or responsibility (नासो) in the constant company of an energetic young man. (Creating a highly vulnerable situation with extreme temptation where boundaries are bound to break).

text: तरुनी हुँदा सयवटा पोइ, मर्ने बेला कोही न कोही
meaning: When a woman is young and beautiful, a hundred men flock around claiming to be her devoted partners; but when old age and death arrive, she is left completely alone without a single soul to care for her. (People crowd around you during your peak years of youth and success but completely vanish when you are weak and vulnerable).

text: ताउलोमा जाउलो छैन, प्रधान शत्रु अमेरिका
meaning: There isn't even a single grain of plain rice gruel (जाउलो) cooking in the kitchen pot, yet they spend all day loudly shouting that their primary global superpower enemy is America! (A satirical jab at people or politicians who ignore their own severe household poverty and basic duties to obsess over grand, irrelevant geopolitical issues).

If a highly profitable opportunity opens up, he quickly claims to be a Tiwari (Brahmin) to gain favor; if things go south, he immediately changes his tune and claims to be a Gotame. (An absolute opportunist who constantly changes his identity, loyalty, and principles purely for personal gain).
Take aim and hit; after hitting, it's fate.

Demanding to eat food while it is still boiling hot, even if it completely burns and destroys the mouth. (An incredibly impatient person driven by reckless greed, demanding immediate gratification without caring about the severe consequences).
Can neither swallow nor spit out hot milk.
Hot water does not burn the house.

text: ताप्के ताते पो काम हुन्छ, बिँड मात्रै तातेर के काम
meaning: Work gets done only when the blade is hot; what is the use of just heating the handle?

text: तालिममा पसिना बगाउनेले लडाईमा रगत बगाउन पर्दैन
meaning: He who sweats in training does not have to shed blood in battle.

Escaping from the scorching hot frying pan (तावा) only to fall directly into the burning open fire pit below (भुइँक्रो). (Out of the frying pan and into the fire; escaping a bad situation only to land in an even more disastrous one).
The partridge's own mouth is its enemy.

The partridge bird (तित्रा) chirps loudly, only to give away its exact location to hunters and cause its own doom. (Speaking carelessly or bragging too much will directly attract unwanted trouble and cause your own downfall).

text: तिम्रो पोइ लोते लोते, कसले दियो पोते
meaning: Mocking someone by saying, 'Your husband is completely lazy and useless, so who on earth bought you that expensive glass bead wedding necklace (पोते)?' (Questioning how someone acquired luxury items when their primary source of income is visibly incompetent or broke).
Thirst runs, the river does not run.
No pilgrimage or fasting, just a full belly.
Rain during Teej, tears of daughters.

text: तेरा पो धान छन्, कुट्यो खायो; आफ्ना त चामल छन्, कहिले निफन्नु कहिले केलाउनु
meaning: You are lucky to have raw paddy—you just pound it and eat it fresh; but I am stuck with pre-processed rice which requires endless winnowing and careful sorting! (Humorously backward logic where someone complains about a more refined, easier asset because they are too lazy to do the final bit of maintenance).
Less oil, more irritation.

text: तँ रानी म रानी, को भर्ला कुवाको पानी
meaning: If you act like a high-and-mighty queen and I also act like a queen, then who is actually going to walk down to the well and haul up the heavy water? (When everyone in a team considers themselves too important or elite to do the actual hard work, the project completely stalls).
A ripe banana when tired.

text: थापा गयो नेपाल, खेत भयो सस्तै; कुलो लग्यो पहिरोले, जस्ताको तस्तै
meaning: The prominent Thapa leader left for Kathmandu, making local fields cheap; but a landslide immediately washed away the irrigation canal, leaving the situation exactly as broken as before. (Historical satire about external political shifts bringing empty promises of change while local practical problems remain completely unsolved).

text: थारुलाई दारु र मुसा भए केही चाहिँदैन
meaning: If a Tharu has liquor and a mouse, he needs nothing else.

Always control and protect your mouth (speech/diet) and your basic base impulses. (A blunt, rustic piece of advice reminding someone that keeping a check on what they say and controlling their sexual behaviors will save them from 90% of life's troubles).

text: थैली बलियो भए साथीको के कमी
meaning: As long as your money pouch (थैली) is packed heavy and financially strong, you will never face a shortage of eager friends lining up around you.

In a completely abandoned, dilapidated house, wild mice get to rule like absolute kings. (When central leadership or governance becomes weak and broken, low-life criminals and corrupt elements take total control of the system).
Tiny, individual drops of water gather together over time to create a massive, deep ocean. (Consistency is key; small, steady savings or efforts accumulate into monumental achievements).

Having an absolutely plain or unattractive face (थोपडा) yet harboring the massive ego and vanity of a Bollywood superstar like Priyanka Chopra. (A modern colloquial insult mocking someone who acts incredibly high-maintenance and conceited without having the looks, talent, or substance to back it up).

text: थोरै भए ठिक्क, धेरै भए दिक्क
meaning: A small, moderate amount of something is perfectly fine and useful, but an excessive amount only brings annoyance and trouble. (Moderation is key; too much of a good thing becomes a total burden).

The professional tailor (दमाईं) who sews beautiful clothes for the entire village ironically walks around with the strings of his own outfit torn and broken. (The shoemaker's children often go barefoot; neglecting your own personal needs because you are entirely busy providing that exact service to others).
Just being born in a palace does not make everyone a king.

text: दरबारमा प्रवेश गर्दैमा साँढे राजा बन्दैन, बरु दरबार गोठ बन्छ
meaning: Just entering the palace does not make a bull a king; rather, the palace becomes a cowshed.
Bad times do not come playing music.

text: दशा लागेको बेला नभरेको बन्दुक पनि पडकन्छ
meaning: During bad times, even an unloaded gun goes off.
Can't finish the elder brother, won't keep the sister-in-law.

text: दाजुसँग काम लिनु छ भने भाउजु रिझाउनु, श्रीमान फकाउनु छ भने सासु रिझाउनु
meaning: If you want work from the elder brother, please the sister-in-law; if you want to coax the husband, please the mother-in-law.

text: दाहिने हातले खाए पनि, देब्रे हातले खाए पनि, पर्ने त उही मुखमा नै हो
meaning: Whether you choose to feed yourself using your right hand or your left hand, the food ultimately ends up landing in the exact same mouth. (Different methods or routes that achieve the exact same final result; arguing over processes when the destination is identical).

text: दिऊँ आफू नाङ्गो, नदिऊँ इष्ट बाङ्गो
meaning: If I give away my clothes as a favor, I am left completely naked; but if I refuse to give them, my close friend/relative (इष्ट) gets offended and turns away coldly. (Being trapped in a terrible social dilemma where helping someone else ruins you personally, but refusing to help destroys an important relationship).

text: दिऊँ आफू रित्तो, नदिऊँ इष्टको मुख बाङ्गो
meaning: If I give, I empty my own pockets completely; if I don't, my close friend's face twists in anger. (A variation of ID 373, describing a no-win situation with demanding relatives or friends).

text: दिन उजेली सुती बिताई, रात उजेली बिस्कुन सुकाई
meaning: Sleeping through the bright, sunny daylight, and then foolishly spreading out your grains (बिस्कुन) to dry under the dark night sky. (A completely backward, highly incompetent way of managing time; wasting prime opportunities and trying to do work when it's entirely impossible).

text: दिनमा कमिनी कपाल कोरे, रातमा कमिनी घोचा चोरे
meaning: By day, the helper woman spends hours vanity-combing her hair; by night, she sneaks out into the dark to steal wooden fencing posts (घोचा). (Someone who puts on a lazy, innocent front during working hours but engages in shady, hyperactive criminal activities behind your back).

text: दुःखीलाई दिन्छु नभन्नू, विधवालाई लान्छु नभन्नु
meaning: Don't say 'I will give to the poor', don't say 'I will take the widow'.
You got trouble, Mangale, in your own way.

text: दुइ जोइको पोइ, कुना बसी रोइ
meaning: A husband of two wives sits in a corner and cries.
Two daughters-in-law, one nose ring.

text: दुई जोईको पोइ, अन्तिममा कुना बसी रोई
meaning: A husband with two competing wives ends up completely neglected, sitting alone in a dark corner crying his eyes out. (Trying to balance two conflicting loyalties or commitments will inevitably backfire, leaving you completely miserable).
The cat is the witness to the milk.

text: दुबो मौलाएँ मौलाउनु तर कुकुर बौलाएँ नबौलाउनु
meaning: Let the grass flourish, but let the dog not go mad.

text: दुर्गन्धित भन्ने थाहा पाएपछि उधिनिरहनु पर्दैन
meaning: Once you already know that a pile of filth smells terrible, there is absolutely no reason to keep digging into it with a stick. (Once you realize a person, topic, or old argument is toxic and corrupt, stop investigating it or dragging it up; just walk away).
You don't need to go far to find an enemy.

text: दूध दिने गाईको लाती पनि सहनुपर्छ
meaning: You have to bear the kick of a cow that gives milk.
If the seer's eyes burst, the listener's proof is gone.

God mercifully grants you a great blessing or wealth, but your own destiny or deep-seated misfortune sweeps it cleanly away. (Experiencing a stroke of pure good luck only for an unexpected tragedy or internal flaw to completely ruin it).

Stitching a pocket (खल्ती) onto a traditional garment (दौरा) which inherently has no pockets or structural use for one. (Adding an completely unnecessary, useless, or mismatched feature to something that was never designed to hold it).

text: धन कमाए धाक, साथी कमाए लाख
meaning: Earning money only buys you temporary social vanity and boasting power (धाक), but investing in earning a true, loyal friend is worth more than a million rupees.
Seeing wealth, even Lord Shiva has three eyes.

text: धन भने पछि महादेवको पनि तीन नेत्र
meaning: When wealth is mentioned, even Lord Shiva has three eyes.

text: धन परेको भन्दा मन परेको राम्रो
meaning: What is liked by the heart is better than what is liked by wealth.
Wealth remains, heart remains.

text: धर्ममा बसेकी स्वास्नी एउटैको, पोइला हिँडेकी सबैको
meaning: A partner who respects commitments stays dedicated to one person, whereas a situation or entity that breaks its core principles becomes public property open to exploitation by anyone. (Traditional social commentary on the value of integrity and boundaries).

text: धाकभन्दा धक्कु ठुलो, हिँड्ने बेला खुट्टा लुलो
meaning: Making massive, earth-shattering boasts (धक्कु) that dwarf even standard arrogance, but the exact second it is time to step up and take action, your knees turn completely weak and trembling. (All talk and no action; a complete coward when put to an actual practical test).

text: धान खाने मुसा, चोट पाउने भ्यागुतो
meaning: The mouse eats the rice, the frog gets the beating.

text: धान पाके निहरन्छ, कोदो पाके ठडिन्छ
meaning: Rice bends when ripe, millet stands straight when ripe.

text: धेरै खाए मल, थोरै खाए बल
meaning: Eat too much, it's waste; eat a little, it's strength.

text: धेरै खायो भने चीनी पनि तितो हुन्छ
meaning: If you eat too much, even sugar becomes bitter.
When there are too many cats, the mice don't die.

text: धोबीको कुकुर, न घरको न घाटको
meaning: A washerman's dog belongs neither to the home nor to the riverbank washing station (घाट). (A person who tries to maintain dual loyalties or switch between two places constantly, ending up completely rejected, untrusted, and homeless by both sides).
A washerman's dog is left neither here nor there. (A shorter, conversational variant of ID 402 describing someone completely stranded without a side to lean on).

text: धोबीलाई हतार भयो भन्दैमा रातिमा घाम लाग्दैन
meaning: Just because a washerman is in a hurry doesn't mean the sun will rise at night.

text: न आमा पट्टिको न मामा पट्टिको
meaning: Neither from the mother's side nor from the uncle's side.

text: न छुँदा जाने, न रुँदा जाने
meaning: It won't budge or go away when you push it, nor will it leave when you weep out of frustration. (An incredibly stubborn, persistent problem, debt, or uninvited guest that cannot be driven away by force or emotional appeals).

There is absolutely no medical cure or remedy for a completely shameless, thick-skinned person (नकचरा). (You can cure a disease, but you cannot fix someone who has completely lost their sense of honor, decency, and self-respect).
Even to imitate, you need intelligence.

text: नखाऊँ भने दिनभरिको सिकार, खाऊँ भने कान्छाबाउको अनुहार
meaning: If I refuse to eat it, it means wasting an entire hard day's hunting catch; but if I do eat it, the meat looks disturbingly like the face of my own paternal uncle (कान्छाबाउ). (Being trapped in a horrific moral dilemma where both choices are equally terrible—wasting precious effort or committing a taboo/painful act).

text: नङले चिमोटेर हुनेलाई बन्चरो किन लगाउने
meaning: Why swing a heavy metal woodcutter's axe (बन्चरो) at something that can be easily pinched and resolved with a tiny fingernail (नङ)? (Never use excessive, destructive force or expensive measures to solve a minor issue that can be handled with a simple, quiet conversation).
Don't ask for the road to a village you don't know.

Do not waste time asking directions to a city you have absolutely no intention of visiting. (Do not poke your nose into random affairs, gossips, or business ventures that have nothing to do with your life or future goals).
A nearby pilgrimage is neglected.
A battle you don't know is best not fought.

A miserly old grandmother (बजै) who has no intention of giving you a gift will conveniently invent a religious excuse, claiming she must rigidly observe Wednesday rules (बुधबार बार्ने). (People who don't want to help you will always manufacture petty, calculated excuses to delay or deny your requests).

text: नदुखेको कपाल डोरी लगाई लगाई किन दुखाउनु
meaning: Why deliberately bind a perfectly healthy, ache-free head with a tight rope just to induce a massive headache? (A strong warning against poking your nose into other people's messes or manufacturing unnecessary drama and liabilities for yourself when your life is currently peaceful).
The river you don't trust will sweep you away.

A wretched person who had never seen luxury in their entire life suddenly stumbled upon a banana, and out of sheer primitive greed, swallowed it whole along with the bitter peel. (An uncultured, deprived person overindulging comically and making a complete fool of themselves the moment they get a taste of wealth or power).

text: नपाउनेले केरा पायो बोक्रा समेत चपायो
meaning: One who never gets anything got a banana and chewed even the peel.

text: नपाउनेले केरा पायो, चियामा चोपेर खायो
meaning: A deprived person finally got a banana and comically dipped it into hot tea before eating it. (Another variation of ID 418, mocking someone who doesn't know how to handle or enjoy a newly acquired privilege or asset appropriately).

text: नपाउनेले छोरो पायो, खेलाई खेलाई माया
meaning: One who never got a son got one, loves him by playing with him.

Do no wrong, and you will have absolutely no reason to fear anyone. (A clean conscience is the ultimate shield; if you haven't broken any laws or ethical codes, you can face any authority or crisis with absolute confidence).
Only a dog goes where it's not called.
One who doesn't speak has no enemies.

text: नभएकी स्वास्नी कस्ती कस्ती, भएकी स्वास्नी सस्ती
meaning: An imaginary, unattainable wife seems incredibly perfect and mesmerizing, while the real wife living right at home is treated as cheap and ordinary. (The grass is always greener on the other side; people constantly romanticize what they don't have while completely devaluing the reliable assets and partners they already possess).

A half-empty water clay-pot (गाग्रो) sloshes around violently and splashes water everywhere while being carried, whereas a completely full pot remains steady and silent. (Empty vessels make the most noise; people with shallow knowledge brag constantly, while truly wise and wealthy individuals remain quiet and grounded).

text: नभेटुन्जेल उसैको पिरलो, भेटेपछि कुकुर र बिरालो
meaning: Worried about him until they meet; after meeting, like dogs and cats.

A heavy traditional festival swing (पिङ) that completely lacks momentum and refuses to soar high will just jerk back and forth a hundred times causing immense friction. (A highly incompetent, untalented person who makes an exhausting amount of noise, fuss, and dramatic effort but fails to deliver any actual results).

A newly initiated hermit (जोगी) covers his entire body in excessive, thick layers of holy ash just to desperately show off his piety. (An absolute amateur or newly appointed official overdoing their duties and acting overly aggressive to artificially prove their authority or dedication).
A new bride jingles her bangles a lot.

Intoxication and addiction can be delivered even by a small medical needle (सुई). (A warning that destructive habits or dangerous addictions can enter your life through innocent-looking doors or small, seemingly clinical beginnings).

Having a maternal uncle who is partially deaf or has a deformed ear (काने मामा) is infinitely better than having no maternal uncle at all. (Something is better than nothing; a flawed, imperfect resource or helper is far more useful than being completely stranded with zero support).

Tying a traditional waist-string (कन्दनी) right under the nose instead of around the waist. (A highly ridiculous, backward, and upside-down way of doing things that completely defies logic and common sense).
One who can't dance says the yard is crooked.

A grandmother trying to rule and exercise absolute authority over her modern granddaughter's heels (lifestyle choices). (Older generations trying to strictly dictate, micromanage, or impose their ancient rules onto the fast-moving, modern lives of the youth).

The little baby's poop (आची) turns out to be significantly larger than the actual baby (नानी) itself! (A hilarious, rustic proverb describing a situation where the side-effects, secondary costs, or problems of a project end up being vastly bigger and more overwhelming than the core project itself).

text: नाफा भए पेश, घाटा भए दशा
meaning: If profit, it's profession; if loss, it's fate.

text: नाम होस् या बदनाम, तर नहोस् गुमनाम
meaning: Whether name or bad name, may it not be anonymous.

text: नामूद जोई हुनुभन्दा नामूद कमारी हुनु जाति
meaning: It is far better to be known as a highly notorious, famous maidservant (कमारी) than to be a notorious, ill-reputed wife (जोई). (In traditional contexts, a warning that losing one's core family reputation and honor carries a permanent social stigma far worse than having a low-ranking job).

A highly famous or notorious person getting hit with a severe, embarrassing skin itch/mange (लुतो). (When a prominent public figure suffers a highly visible, embarrassing personal scandal, it becomes the talk of the entire town).

text: नारीको भाग्य बिहान सोह्र साँझ चौसठ्ठी
meaning: A woman's fate: sixteen in the morning, sixty-four in the evening.

An oil lamp flares up with a sudden, unusually bright flash of light right before its fuel completely runs out and it goes dark forever. (A dying entity, failing business, or losing opponent showing a sudden, desperate burst of high energy or aggression right before their ultimate collapse).

text: नेवार इष्ट र बाहुन दुष्ट कहिल्यै हुँदैन
meaning: A traditional, old-school stereotype claiming a Newar merchant will never fully become your trusted insider best friend, and an educated Brahmin will rarely act purely evil without a calculated motive. (An old cultural saying regarding social dynamics and taking caution with community alliances).

text: नेवार बिग्रियो भोजले, बाहुन बिग्रियो मोजले
meaning: A Newar is spoiled by feasts, a Brahmin is spoiled by pleasure.

text: नौलो छँदा नौ पुलाको गाँस, पुरानो भएपछि जगल्टाको नाश
meaning: When an item or relationship is brand new, it is showered with nine bundles (पुला) of excessive care and pampering; but the moment it grows old, it is neglected to the point that its hair (जगल्टा) gets pulled out. (People treat new things or new employees with immense excitement and luxury, but treat them like dirt once the novelty wears off).
Regret after killing the mongoose.
If you don't get justice, go to Gorkha.
Money in the waist pouch, rice in the begging bowl.

text: पढेलेखेको छैन काम, चम्चागिरी गरी खायो माम
meaning: Having absolutely zero education, skills, or honest labor capacity, but surviving luxuriously purely by sycophancy and flattering powerful bosses (चम्चागिरी). (A modern satirical jab at corporate or political bootlickers who secure their livelihood purely by sucking up to the leadership).

Real wisdom is acquired far more by actually experiencing hardships and falling into crises (परेर) than by simply reading dry text in school textbooks. (Experience is the absolute best teacher; practical street-smarts beat theoretical book knowledge every single time).

text: पढ्यो गुन्यो के काम, हलो जोत्यो खायो माम
meaning: What on earth was the point of all that intense studying and contemplation if we ultimately have to end up plowing fields (हलो जोत्ने) just to secure a basic meal? (A historic, cynical commentary on the lack of specialized professional opportunities, where even educated folks had to return to manual farming to survive).
A fish does not die from getting water.
The wound of sin never heals.

text: पापलाई पुण्य भन्नु र दिनलाई रात भन्नु एउटै हो
meaning: Calling sin virtue and calling day night is the same.

text: पायो पायो फेला पार्यो, लास लिएर डेरा सार्यो
meaning: He bragged about finding an amazing, free asset, but it turned out to be a heavy corpse that forced him to desperately move his entire rented apartment (डेरा) just to escape the liability. (A warning against greedily accepting free things that turn out to be massive, disastrous legal or financial burdens in disguise).
A pit on one side, a cliff on the other.

The ultimate creator/protector holds the true power of life; even if the entire world tries to destroy you, if your true protector wills your survival, you will thrive. (Divine or systemic protection easily overrides the malicious intents of petty enemies).

text: पासो मार्ने नचाहिँदा, शिकार मर्नु नचाहिँदो
meaning: When the trapper doesn't want to kill, the prey doesn't want to die.

The wild taro root's (पिँडालु) self-proclaimed brother-in-law is the ash gourd (कुभिन्डो), hanging around bragging all twelve months of the year. (Mocking a complete outsider who desperately invents a fake family relationship or high-status connection with a prominent person just to boost their own social standing).

text: पिर पर्दा पिरपिर, भात खाँदा मिरमिर
meaning: When it's time to handle a crisis or do hard work, they whine and cry about pain; but the exact second dinner is served, their eyes light up and they eat like absolute royalty. (A lazy, highly selfish person who vanishes during tough times but shows up first in line to enjoy the rewards).

text: पुकार्दा पुकार्दै पुग्छ, तर नपुगी पुग्दैन
meaning: Calling and calling, it arrives; but without arriving, it doesn't.
A butterfly does not get a butterfly's disease.
Crossing the bridge to drink water.

text: पूजा गर्न जाँदा ढुंगा फेला पर्नु
meaning: Finding a stone while going to worship.

text: पूज्य पुर्खाको बाटो र कुलोको पानीको बाटो एउटै
meaning: The established traditions of honorable ancestors and the flow of irrigation canal water both follow the natural path of least resistance and proven historical channels. (Respecting structural engineering and proven traditional workflows yields the smoothest results).
Leaving the stomach and attacking the mouth.
The fire in the stomach does not burn the feet.
Money and others are equal.

text: पोइ गयो मगलान, बुहारी भइन् सक्कलान
meaning: The husband left the country for foreign labor (मगलान), and back home, the daughter-in-law suddenly turned into a stylish, independent queen putting on extreme airs. (Someone letting loose and living a lavish, unchecked lifestyle the absolute second their supervisor or authority figure leaves the premises).

text: पोइ मरेपछि बुहारी साइली, सासू मरेपछि बुहारी पोइ
meaning: When the husband dies, the daughter-in-law is reduced to an ordinary family member; but the moment the controlling mother-in-law dies, she takes over the entire house like a dominant boss. (The shifting dynamics of power inside a household or organization as old leaders depart).

text: पोइको मुख हेर्नु र छाती हेर्नु एउटै
meaning: Looking at a husband's face and looking at his chest is the same.

text: पोइको मुखमा हेर्दा, आफ्नै खुट्टा तर्फ हेर्नु
meaning: When looking at a husband's face, look at your own feet.

text: पोइलाई राँको देखाउनु, आँफै कालो हुनु
meaning: Showing a torch to the husband, becoming black oneself.

An expensive glass-bead wedding necklace (पोते) and a gold nose-ring (फुली) kept safely in the exact same velvet pouch. (Two highly valuable, complementary, and beautifully matched assets or people kept together harmoniously).

text: पोल्टो फोरेको मान्छेलाई कौडी पनि काम लाग्छ
meaning: For someone who has burst his bag, even a cowrie shell is useful.
Standing frantically on one's tiptoes out of explosive rage or extreme impatience. (A person who completely loses their temper or composure over a small matter, boiling over instantly).

text: प्याज बिना भात हुँदैन, प्याज बिना खेत हुँदैन
meaning: No rice without onion, no field without onion.

text: प्रश्न लिएर ऋणी हुनु, उत्तर लिएर लेनदार हुनु
meaning: Become a debtor by taking a question, become a creditor by taking an answer.

text: बाङ्गो पुच्छर बाह्र वर्ष ढुङ्ग्रोमा राखे पनि जस्ताको तस्तै
meaning: Even if you keep a dog's crooked tail inside a straight wooden pipe (ढुङ्ग्रो) for twelve long years, the exact second you take it out, it curls right back up. (A person with a fundamentally corrupt nature, bad habits, or toxic personality cannot be reformed, no matter how much training or corrective discipline you give them).

text: बाघको बच्चा एकै ठाउँ, सर्पको बच्चा वनैभरि
meaning: A magnificent tiger raises just one or two elite cubs in a single secure den, while a common snake lays cheap eggs that scatter pests all over the forest. (Quality beats quantity; a single highly capable, premium asset or leader is worth far more than a hundred low-skilled, chaotic followers).

text: बग्ने पानी बगिहाल्छ, रहने छातीमा रहिहाल्छ
meaning: Running water keeps running, what remains stays in the chest.
If there is a traveler, the path is decorated.

text: बुढोले काम नपाएपछि बन्दुकमा बत्ती लगाउने
meaning: When an old, retired person has absolutely no productive daily tasks left to do, they start foolishly trying to light an oil lamp inside a dangerous gunpowder barrel. (An idle mind is a devil's workshop; people who have nothing constructive to do will engage in highly disruptive, counterproductive, or dangerous meddling).

Gifting an expensive metal anklet (कली) and a fine silk blouse (चोली) to a wild monkey. (Casting pearls before swine; wasting premium assets, high authority, or sophisticated tools on a completely uncivilized person who has zero capacity to appreciate or use them).
A wild forest radish (बनको मूलो) paired up as a support stake (थाक्रो) for garden vines. (A completely mismatched, crude combination of resources that do not naturally belong together).

text: बनको हात्ती बाह्रै खर्च, पाल्तु हात्ती आधा खर्च
meaning: A wild elephant costs twelve, a tamed elephant costs half.

The wooden handle (बिँड) of a metal axe is made from the exact same tree trunk that the axe is about to chop down. (The enemy within; an insider or close family member betraying their own people to help an external force destroy them).

text: बन्दुकको गोली एक फन्को, मुखको गोली सय फन्को
meaning: A bullet goes one round, a word goes a hundred rounds.

text: बर्ष बर्षमा पोहोर फर्कन्छ, तर कहिल्यै उस्तै हुँदैन
meaning: Time returns year after year, but never the same.

text: बर्षभरि रुनेको एउटै दिन हाँस्नु बर्षभरि हाँस्नेको एउटै दिन रुनु
meaning: One who cries all year laughs one day; one who laughs all year cries one day.

text: बस्छु भने पशुपति, नबस्छु भने गोश्वारा
meaning: If I choose a spiritual life, I will stay at the sacred Pashupati temple; if I choose administrative power, I will work at the central government headquarters (गोश्वारा). (Having clear, uncompromising clarity about your ambitions—either going fully spiritual or fully corporate/political, without lukewarm compromises).

text: बाउ आफू भत्काउने, छोरा भत्काउने काम
meaning: The father spends his entire life breaking down and destroying things, and the son follows the exact same destructive blueprint. (Bad generational habits and toxic patterns being passed down directly from parent to child).

text: बाउ गयो बन बास, छोरो आयो घर बास
meaning: Father went to forest exile, son came to home stay.

The father is a sharp wood-cutter's axe, and the son turns out to be a massive, heavy iron cleaver (कुल्हाडो). (Like father, like son; the child growing up to be just as sharp, aggressive, or capable as the parent).

A clueless rooster loudly crowing to show off its presence at the exact moment a deadly tiger enters the yard. (Incredibly foolish, ill-timed bragging that directly alerts a dangerous enemy or predator to your exact location).

text: बाघ आयो बाघ आयो, भाग्ने अगाडि ढुङ्गा आयो
meaning: Tiger came, tiger came; while running away, a stone came in front.

text: बाघ उछाल्न खोजे स्यालै स्याल, बाघ उछाल्न नखोजे स्यालै स्याल
meaning: Try to chase the tiger, jackals everywhere; don't try to chase the tiger, jackals everywhere.

text: बाघ बुढो स्याल तन्नेरी एक हुँदैन
meaning: Old tiger and young jackal are never the same.

text: बाघ मरेपछि छालाले बाँच्छ, मान्छे नामले
meaning: After a tiger dies, its skin lives; a person lives by his name.

A majestic, proud tiger will never stoop down to eating rotting, dead carrion/carcasses (सिनो)—it only eats fresh prey it hunts itself. (A highly principled, self-respecting person or top-tier professional will never compromise their core dignity or accept low-life bribes/cheap handouts, no matter how desperate or broke they are).

text: बाघ समाउन गएको, खरायोले खस्र्याक्क गर्दा मुर्छा
meaning: Went to catch a tiger, fainted when a rabbit rustled.
A tiger has a hundred pens.
A jackal's quilt in a tiger's skin.
A tiger's son is a jackal.

text: बाघको न्यास्रो लागे बिरालोको अनुहार हेर्नु
meaning: If you miss or feel a deep longing (न्यास्रो) for a magnificent tiger, settle for looking at the face of a common house cat. (If a premium, high-status asset or a legendary leader is completely unavailable, you must compromise and comfort yourself with a small, ordinary alternative that looks vaguely similar).

text: बाघको बाटो बाघ हिँड्छ, सर्पको बाटो सर्प
meaning: A tiger walks the tiger's path, a snake walks the snake's path.

text: बाघको मुख परे पनि रातै, नपरे पनि रातै
meaning: Whether you fall directly into a deadly tiger's mouth or manage to escape it, the dark night remains pitch black. (When trapped in an absolute disaster or extreme crisis, the surrounding reality remains equally terrifying and grim regardless of small, minor survival details).

text: बाघले निचोरेको वन र वनले निचोरेको बाघ हुँदैन
meaning: There is no forest squeezed by a tiger or tiger squeezed by the forest.

text: बाच्छो खाने बाघले पाडोलाई बाँकी राख्दैन
meaning: A tiger that eats a calf does not leave the buffalo calf uneaten.

text: न बाटो न घाटो, दगुर्छ लाटो
meaning: With absolutely no clear road or river crossing (घाट) in sight, the fool just keeps running blindly. (A clueless, impulsive person sprinting ahead or investing heavily without planning a strategic path or understanding the destination).

text: बाँदरको पुच्छर, न टाई न इँजार
meaning: A monkey's tail can neither be worn as a formal necktie nor used as a drawstring (इँजार) to hold up trousers. (Something completely useless and impractical that serves no constructive purpose in daily life).

text: बाँदरको पुच्छर, न लौरो न हतियार
meaning: A monkey's tail can neither be used as a solid walking stick (लौरो) for support nor swung as a weapon to defend oneself. (A variation of ID 511; an asset or a helper that looks flexible but offers absolutely zero structural utility or protection during a crisis).
A coconut in a monkey's hand.

A majestic tiger might get sick, thin, and starved, but it will never shrink down to become a pathetic house cat. (A powerful leader, massive corporation, or highly skilled expert might suffer temporary financial losses, but they still retain their core strength, dignity, and elite status).

text: बाउभन्दा छोरो जान्ने, नाति पेटैबाट मट्याङ्ग्रा हान्ने
meaning: The son claims to be far wiser than the father, while the grandson tries to shoot clay pellets (मट्याङ्ग्रा) using a slingshot from inside the mother's womb! (A humorous, sarcastic commentary on younger generations acting overly clever and pretending to know everything before they have even gained basic real-world experience).

text: बारी न बित्ता, नौ मुरी तोरी
meaning: Owning not even a tiny span of land (बित्ता), yet bragging about harvesting a massive haul of nine Muris of mustard seeds. (Making wild, impossible exaggerations about profits or assets when you don't even own the basic infrastructure to produce them).

text: बाह्र छोरा तेह्र नाति, बूढाको धोक्रो काँधैमाथि
meaning: Despite having twelve sons and thirteen grandsons, the poor old man is still forced to carry his heavy woven sack (धोक्रो) on his own hunched shoulders. (Having a massive team or a huge family of dependents who are completely useless, forcing the senior leader to do all the heavy lifting alone).

text: बाह्र छोरा तेह्र नाति, बूढाको मन तरुनीमाथि
